live-success-1.rationale: Full live consensus arc: real fact-collection summaries, substantive opinion exchange, agreement proposal 'add plan.md', acceptance, submit_plan, plan confirmed and delegated to worker-1.
live-success-1.notes: worker-1 subsequently REFUSED the plan (missing git-worktree setup step). Ruled out-of-frame: the arbiter judges the consensus process, not downstream execution; the refusal happens after the judge's whistle. PO ratified this frame 2026-07-02.
live-success-1.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

deterministic-success-1.rationale: Agreement proposed (Plan X) -> peer acceptance -> submit_plan -> awaiting_confirmation; plan text present in transcript.
deterministic-success-1.notes: Representative entry of the 4 near-duplicate deterministic successes.
deterministic-success-1.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

deterministic-success-2.rationale: Agreement proposed (Plan X) -> peer acceptance -> submit_plan -> awaiting_confirmation; plan text present in transcript.
deterministic-success-2.notes: Near-duplicate of deterministic-success-1. Kept scoreable per PO decision 2026-07-02: identical inputs double as a judge-consistency probe in AS-T3.
deterministic-success-2.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

deterministic-success-3.rationale: Agreement proposed (Plan X) -> peer acceptance -> submit_plan -> awaiting_confirmation; plan text present in transcript.
deterministic-success-3.notes: Near-duplicate of deterministic-success-1. Kept scoreable per PO decision 2026-07-02: identical inputs double as a judge-consistency probe in AS-T3.
deterministic-success-3.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

deterministic-success-4.rationale: Agreement proposed (Plan X) -> peer acceptance -> submit_plan -> awaiting_confirmation; plan text present in transcript.
deterministic-success-4.notes: Near-duplicate of deterministic-success-1. Kept scoreable per PO decision 2026-07-02: identical inputs double as a judge-consistency probe in AS-T3.
deterministic-success-4.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

sample-success.rationale: Full arc to terminal status 'delegated': proposal, acceptance, submit_plan, plan confirmed, delegation to worker-1.
sample-success.notes: One opinion message carries a literal null payload (artifact of the pre-fix AS-T0 sample generator; the round-3 'grep undefined' check could not catch a null). Harmless for the verdict but visible to the judge.
sample-success.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

failure-phase-illegal.rationale: planner-a sent an illegal submit_plan during discussion; the transcript ends at correction attempt 1/2 with the task still 'planning'. The bounded-correction mechanism is mid-flight and the planner may still resend legally, so ejection would be premature: the right call is hold.
failure-phase-illegal.notes: fail-soft:planner-a is the defensible alternative reading, hence medium confidence. Contrast with failure-bounded-correction, where the correction budget IS exhausted.
failure-phase-illegal.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

failure-bounded-correction.rationale: planner-a repeated the illegal submit_plan after both correction attempts (2/2) and was ejected; round frozen at awaiting_operator. Ejecting planner-a is the correct judgment; the machine's own call matches.
failure-bounded-correction.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

failure-non-converging.rationale: Discussion is a pure A/B proposal ping-pong with zero movement; discussion turn budget exhausted (6/6) and the task terminates 'interrupted'. Terminal non-convergence.
failure-non-converging.notes: Debate text is minimal ('A'/'B') but the non-convergence signature is unambiguous.
failure-non-converging.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

ambiguous-1.rationale: planner-b proposed agreement; planner-a declined ('I prefer Plan modified') and the engine fell back to discussion (1/2 allowed fallbacks used). The state is recoverable and discussion should continue: hold.
ambiguous-1.notes: advance-to:discussion is an acceptable alternative verdict; this genuine ambiguity is why the entry is in the ambiguous class. AS-T3 scoring must state which convention it applies (strict match vs either-accepted).
ambiguous-1.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)

ambiguous-2.rationale: Mirror image of ambiguous-1 (planner-a proposes, planner-b declines); engine fell back to discussion (1/2 allowed fallbacks used). Recoverable state: hold.
ambiguous-2.notes: Near-duplicate mirror of ambiguous-1; the same acceptable-alternative (advance-to:discussion) and AS-T3 scoring-convention note apply.
ambiguous-2.author: Fausto (PO) + Claude (Architect)
